mission.title: open-webui: feat: custom themes
mission.description: feat: custom themes. Requested by 9+ users.

steps.title: Check current open-webui deployment
steps.description:
Verify your open-webui version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
helm list -n open-webui 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working open-webui installation.

steps.title: Review open-webui configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant open-webui configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get configmap -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=open-webui
```
Custom themes would be nice. Here's an example of one I made: https://github.com/rose-pine/ollama-webui

steps.title: Apply the fix for feat: custom themes
steps.description:
We removed it due to it having fallen quite far behind in upkeep. If someone would like to spruce it up, we'd be happy to add it back to the list. As of now, there's no way to _easily_ add custom themes without making changes to the code. It's been suggested as a feature to have some way to mount a
```yaml
<script>
...

const base = 'bg-white min-h-screen';
$: classBase = `${base} ${$$props.class ?? ''}`;

...
</script>

< ... class={classBase} ... >
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get events -n open-webui --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "feat: custom themes" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Open WebUI added custom theme support starting with PR #343, which introduced the Rose Pine theme as the first custom theme. The theming system allows users to customize the UI appearance. While the initial Rose Pine theme was later removed during a UI overhaul, the custom theming infrastructure remains and has evolved to support user-defined themes through the settings interface.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with open-webui installed or the issue environment reproducible.
